generated_text: The group discussed the conceptual design of the remote control, breaking it into three steps: functional design, conceptual design, and detailed design. Functional design focuses on how the remote works and its appearance. Conceptual design involves market research on existing remote controls and specifications for components and user interface. They decided to use a whiteboard for brainstorming and drawing examples like an elephant and a fish, emphasizing user-friendly features and originality. The project included a folder for organization and a smartboard for presentations. The team also considered using different pen colors and thickness for drawing on the whiteboard to demonstrate the remote's functionality.
expected_answer: In order to make a satisfying profit, the group had to set their price at 25 euros, which was relatively high. For fear that the higher price would lead to fewer consumers, they decided to make it a universal remote control, being able to function on different TVs. The group also planned to add a touchscreen onto the remote control and even include some games for children. What’s more, they paid some attention to the energy source, whether it should be a rechargeable one or just use normal batteries.

generated_text: The solution to the major challenge for the transition of excluded students involved developing a continuum of support through local authorities, including the EOTAS network, which works across Wales to provide tiered approaches. In Swansea, local authority numbers increased fourfold over six years, highlighting the need for more capacity and specialist provision. Local authorities like Torfaen and Swansea have implemented strategies, such as the "team-around-the-family" approach, to engage parents and address complex issues, ensuring a whole-family support system. These efforts aim to re-integrate students through improved staff training, trauma-informed practices, and collaboration with social care and health services.
expected_answer: Sharon Davies recommended the team-around-the-family approach to ensure what was needed for learners to go back into school, as well as what the school could provide. She pointed out the importance of the whole package of support, which not only surrounded the learners but also their families. In addition, she gave an example of the PRU in Oxford who built the partnership between the school and the PRU student to demonstrate the responsibility of schools and the significance of school participation.

generated_text: The product is an international remote control aimed at global markets, with a target selling price of 25 Euros, though the product cost must not exceed 12.50 Euros. The team discussed designing a user-friendly, original, and trendy remote control with a focus on functional design, individual work, and meetings. The Industrial Designer and User Interface Designer have distinct roles: the Industrial Designer focuses on the physical design and appearance, while the User Interface Designer handles the user experience and interface. The remote control must be intuitive, easy to use, and adaptable to different keyboard layouts (e.g., QWERTY, AZERTY). The project requires collaboration across roles to ensure the remote control meets both functional and aesthetic design goals.
expected_answer: The remote control had to be original, trendy, easy to use, international and not too expensive.
